data.content_ref: inline:{\"claim\": \"Excessive elite gift‑exchange and monumental patronage generated unsustainable social expectations, eroding ideological legitimacy of palace rule.\", \"mechanism\": \"Systemic sociopolitical instability caused by elite overproduction and ideological fatigue.\", \"scope\": {\"covers\": [\"Mycenaean Greece\",\"Hittite Anatolia\",\"Ugarit\",\"Levantine city‑states\"], \"excludes\": [\"Egypt\"]}, \"forbidden\": [{\"case\": \"Archaeological datasets showing no significant increase in elite burial wealth or monumental construction during the Late Bronze Age.\", \"eval\": \"rubric:std-hist\"}], \"prose_notes\": \"Critical historiographical essay that questions the prevailing narrative of external shock by focusing on internal ideological decay.\"}
